articles.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
articles.summary: The 34th SpaceX commercial resupply mission under contract with NASA is headed to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments after lifting off at 6:05 p.m. EDT Friday on a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida. The SpaceX spacecraft, loaded with nearly 6,500 pounds […]

articles.title: Live Coverage: SpaceX makes another attempt to launch space station cargo mission
articles.summary: Liftoff of the resupply mission, atop a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for 6:05 p.m. EDT (2205 UTC). The Cargo Dragon is loaded with 6,500 pounds of science and supplies for the space station.

articles.title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
articles.summary: After NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover drilled a sample from this rock on April 25, 2026, it withdrew its robotic arm and pulled the entire rock off the surface with it. Engineers spent several days repositioning the arm and vibrating the drill to try and get the rock loose. When it finally detached on May 1, […]

articles.title: Preparing Smile for space
articles.summary: Before Smile can begin studying how Earth responds to the streams of particles and bursts of radiation from the Sun, the spacecraft had to complete an extraordinary journey here on Earth.

articles.title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
articles.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image reveals an enigmatic galaxy with a bright center and a face that hints at spiral structure, yet it holds no obvious spiral arms. Reddish-brown clumps and filaments of dust partially obscure the galaxy’s full face, while red, blue, and orange light from distant galaxies shines through its diffuse outer […]

articles.title: Earth from Space: Quito’s volcanic landscape
OHB Space UK to Begin Work on ESA EnVision Venus Explorer Mission
articles.summary:
Germany’s OHB has signed an agreement with Thales Alenia Space for the assembly, integration, and testing of the European Space Agency’s EnVision spacecraft. The development of the EnVision mission is being led by Thales Alenia Space under a €367 million contract awarded to the company in February 2025. With EnVision, ESA hopes to capture a […]
The post OHB Space UK to Begin Work on ESA EnVision Venus Explorer Mission appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: A Lyrid meteor from orbit
articles.summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the International Space Station turned into meteor chasers as Earth passed through a cloud of dust and small debris left behind by comet Thatcher in 1861.
